name: State Bank Of Bottineau
description_short: Community bank serving Bottineau, ND since 1901, offering checking, savings, mobile banking, and consumer/commercial loans with local ownership and decision-making.
description_long: State Bank of Bottineau is a locally-owned community bank headquartered in Bottineau, North Dakota, with a history spanning over 120 years of service to the region. Founded in 1901, the institution has maintained its commitment to personalized banking and community involvement throughout its operational history. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection for eligible accounts. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial services. Personal offerings include checking and savings accounts (starting at $50 or $15,000), certificates of deposit, IRAs, consumer loans for home, vehicle, and personal expenses, mobile and online banking with bill pay functionality, debit card services with advanced card management features, and electronic statements. Business customers can access commercial and agricultural lending, business checking and savings accounts, and business mobile banking. The institution promotes competitive interest rates across deposit products and provides additional conveniences like online check ordering and mobile check deposit. State Bank of Bottineau distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local ownership and locally-made decisions. The bank specifically markets itself as a "hometown bank" serving the Bottineau community, with relationship-based lending decisions made by commercial/agricultural lenders who understand local business needs. Their digital offerings (mobile app, online banking, mobile deposit, debit card controls) compete with larger institutions while maintaining personalized service. The institution actively engages the community through programs like spring coloring contests. As a small community bank, State Bank of Bottineau is best suited for customers seeking personalized banking relationships and local decision-making rather than those requiring national branch networks or specialized financial products. The bank serves both personal and business customers in the Bottineau region, though its geographic footprint appears limited to a single physical location. Customers should verify service area coverage and product availability, particularly for those relocating from larger metropolitan areas.
services: Savings accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs)
Online banking with bill pay and person-to-person money transfer
Mobile banking app with debit card management and controls
Debit card services with transaction alerts and spending limits
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Locally-owned with 120+ year history serving Bottineau community since 1901
Advanced mobile app debit card management including transaction limits, card on/off controls, and parental/employer monitoring
Mobile check deposit and online banking with bill pay and person-to-person money transfer capabilities
Competitive interest rates promoted on checking, savings, CDs, and IRAs
Relationship-based lending with local commercial/agricultural lenders who understand business needs
Low account opening minimums ($50 for some savings accounts)
cons: Single physical location in Bottineau, ND limits convenience for customers outside the immediate area
Website lacks specific interest rate information—customers must contact or visit to compare CD and savings rates
No mention of overdraft protection, minimum balance requirements, or monthly fees for accounts
Limited information about loan terms, rates, or approval timelines for consumer and commercial products
best_for: Bottineau, ND residents and local business owners seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers prioritizing local decision-making and personalized service over national branch networks
Small to mid-sized agricultural and commercial businesses needing local lending expertise
diagnosis: State Bank of Bottineau is best for Bottineau-area residents and local business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with local decision-making authority. The main caveat is its geographic limitation to a single location and lack of transparent rate information online, requiring direct contact for pricing comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank in Bottineau, ND since 1901 offering checking, savings, mobile banking, and consumer/commercial loans with local decision-making.
